name: Blodeuwedd
description: Blodeuwedd, meaning 'Flower Face' or 'Flower Aspect,' is a complex and tragic figure in Welsh mythology, primarily known from the Fourth Branch of the Mabinogi, *Math fab Mathonwy*. She was created by the powerful magicians Math fab Mathonwy and Gwydion using flowers of the oak, broom, and meadowsweet to be a wife for Lleu Llaw Gyffes. Lleu was under a curse that he could not be killed by any weapon made by man, born of woman, or within a house. Blodeuwedd, being neither born of woman nor of mortal creation, was seen as the perfect solution to provide Lleu with an heir and break the curse's limitations. However, she fell deeply in love with Gronw Pebr, a lord, and together they plotted Lleu's death. She tricked Lleu into revealing the specific circumstances under which he *could* be killed, a spear crafted over a year, only able to harm him at twilight while standing with one foot on a cauldron and one on a goat. After Lleu was struck, he transformed into an eagle and fled. Gwydion eventually found Lleu and restored him to human form. As punishment for her treachery, Gwydion transformed Blodeuwedd into an owl, condemning her to a solitary existence, hated by all other birds, and forever hunting at night. She represents themes of betrayal, feminine power, the dangers of unchecked desire, and the consequences of defying fate. Her story serves as a cautionary tale and highlights the intertwined nature of magic, love, and revenge in Welsh lore. While not inherently a goddess of any particular domain before her creation, she embodies aspects of beauty, transformation, and the darker sides of nature through her association with flowers and her eventual transformation.
subtitle: The Flower Woman, Betrayer, Owl

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Blodeuwedd
keyMyths.description: Math and Gwydion create Blodeuwedd from flowers to be Lleu's wife, circumventing a curse.

keyMyths.title: Blodeuwedd's Betrayal
keyMyths.description: Blodeuwedd falls in love with Gronw Pebr and plots Lleu's death, tricking him into revealing his weakness.

keyMyths.title: The Transformation into an Owl
keyMyths.description: As punishment for her treachery, Gwydion transforms Blodeuwedd into an owl, condemning her to a lonely existence.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
